summary: Descriptive overview of accompliceliability in substantive law: aiding and abetting, natural and probable consequences.
key_points: aiding and abetting is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for accompliceliability are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine accompliceliability in litigation.
The doctrine of accompliceliability has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding accompliceliability requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
